steps.title: Read two points from the boundary line
steps.content: The boundary line (solid black) separates the shaded region from the unshaded region. Reading directly from the graph, the line passes through (0, −13) on the y-axis and (−10, −11) on the left side.

steps.title: Find the equation of the boundary line using point-slope form
steps.content:
First, find the slope: $$m = \frac{-11 - (-13)}{-10 - 0} = \frac{2}{-10} = -\frac{1}{5}$$
Using point (0, −13), the equation is: $$y = -\frac{1}{5}x - 13$$
Multiply both sides by 5 to clear the fraction: $$5y = -x - 65$$
Rearrange to standard form: $$x + 5y = -65$$

steps.title: Identify r and t, then compute r + t
steps.content:
The boundary line has equation $x + 5y = -65$, which matches the form $rx + ty = -65$.
Therefore, $r = 1$ and $t = 5$, giving us: $$r + t = 1 + 5 = 6$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
